intro: Achyronia argyraea, also known as Argemone mexicana, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, belonging to the poppy family (Papaveraceae). The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in relieving pain and reducing inflammation. The bioactive constituents of Achyronia argyraea include sanguinarine, dihydro-sanguinarine, and protopine, which have been found to exhibit medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Achyronia argyraea is often used to prepare various remedies, including tinctures, decoctions, and infusions, which are used to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
